From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Welsh actress (born 1987)

| Aimee-Ffion Edwards | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1987-11-21) 21 November 1987 (age 38)  [Newport](/wiki/Newport,_Wales "Newport, Wales"), Wales |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 2002–present |

**Aimee-Ffion Edwards** (born 21 November 1987) is a Welsh actress. She is best known for her television roles as Esme Shelby in *[Peaky Blinders](/wiki/Peaky_Blinders_(TV_series) "Peaky Blinders (TV series)")* (2013-2017; 2022) and Shirley Dander in *[Slow Horses](/wiki/Slow_Horses "Slow Horses")* (2022- present).

Her video game voice roles include Rachel Baker in *[Everybody's Gone to the Rapture](/wiki/Everybody%27s_Gone_to_the_Rapture "Everybody's Gone to the Rapture")*, Mio in the English dub of *[Xenoblade Chronicles 3](/wiki/Xenoblade_Chronicles_3 "Xenoblade Chronicles 3")*, and as [Ranni the Witch](/wiki/Ranni_the_Witch "Ranni the Witch") in *[Elden Ring](/wiki/Elden_Ring "Elden Ring")*.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aimee-Ffion_Edwards&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Aimee-Ffion Edwards was born in [Newport](/wiki/Newport,_Wales "Newport, Wales"), on 21 November 1987.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Ffion is fluent in English and [Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"), having attended the Welsh-speaking schools Ysgol Gymraeg Casnewydd (Newport Welsh Medium Primary School) and [Ysgol Gyfun Gwynllyw](/wiki/Ysgol_Gyfun_Gwynllyw "Ysgol Gyfun Gwynllyw") (Torfaen Welsh Medium High School). She played for a local youth [rugby](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") team, the only girl on the boys' team, until she was 14. She would often go from her ballet lessons to rugby matches. She studied [A-level](/wiki/A-level "A-level") drama and later joined the National Youth Theatre of Wales.[[2]](#cite_note-WalesOnline2-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aimee-Ffion_Edwards&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Film and television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aimee-Ffion_Edwards&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Film and television")]

Ffion appeared in the 2002 short film *Dŵr Dwfn*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) She was training to be a classical singer when she appeared on the *[Pop Idol](/wiki/Pop_Idol "Pop Idol")*-type [Welsh language](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language") TV show *[Wawffactor](/wiki/Wawffactor "Wawffactor")* in 2006, finishing as runner-up.[[2]](#cite_note-WalesOnline2-2) She made her television debut as the character Sketch in the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") series *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* in 2008.[[2]](#cite_note-WalesOnline2-2) In 2009, she appeared in the *[Casualty](/wiki/Casualty_(TV_series) "Casualty (TV series)")* episode "Stand By Me" as a teenager who finds a replacement speed date in the wards of Holby City Hospital, and ends up involved in a serious gun-related incident. In the first episode of *[Casualty 1909](/wiki/Casualty_1909 "Casualty 1909")*, she played a young prostitute who was being abused by her father.

In 2010, Ffion appeared in an episode of the supernatural BBC drama series *[Being Human](/wiki/Being_Human_(British_TV_series) "Being Human (British TV series)")* as a theatre usher who is also a ghost. In 2011, she appeared in series 2 of the [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") drama series *[Luther](/wiki/Luther_(TV_series) "Luther (TV series)")* as Jenny Jones.[[2]](#cite_note-WalesOnline2-2) In 2012, she appeared in [Sky Atlantic](/wiki/Sky_Atlantic "Sky Atlantic")'s four-part series *Walking And Talking*, a spin-off from an episode of [Sky One](/wiki/Sky_One "Sky One")'s Christmas series of shorts *[Little Crackers](/wiki/Little_Crackers "Little Crackers")*. In 2013, 2014, and 2021, she appeared as [Esme Shelby](/wiki/Esme_Shelby "Esme Shelby") in the BBC series *[Peaky Blinders](/wiki/Peaky_Blinders_(TV_series) "Peaky Blinders (TV series)")*. In 2014, she appeared in two [BBC Cymru Wales](/wiki/BBC_Cymru_Wales "BBC Cymru Wales") television productions celebrating the centenary of [Dylan Thomas](/wiki/Dylan_Thomas "Dylan Thomas"): as part of an all-Welsh cast in a television adaptation of Thomas' radio drama *[Under Milk Wood](/wiki/Under_Milk_Wood "Under Milk Wood")*, playing the Laugharne Voice,[[4]](#cite_note-4) and as Marianne in *[A Poet in New York](/wiki/A_Poet_in_New_York "A Poet in New York")*, [Andrew Davies](/wiki/Andrew_Davies_(writer) "Andrew Davies (writer)")' dramatisation of Thomas' last days.[[5]](#cite_note-Poet-5)

In 2014, Ffion appeared as Katy in the *[Inside No. 9](/wiki/Inside_No._9 "Inside No. 9")* episode "[The Harrowing](/wiki/The_Harrowing_(Inside_No._9) "The Harrowing (Inside No. 9)")".[[6]](#cite_note-WalesOnline-6) From 2014 to 2015, she played Sophie in the [BBC Four](/wiki/BBC_Four "BBC Four") comedy series *[Detectorists](/wiki/Detectorists "Detectorists")*.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) In 2016, she appeared in *[Death in Paradise](/wiki/Death_in_Paradise_(TV_series) "Death in Paradise (TV series)")* as Sian. In 2017, she appeared in the Channel 4 comedy series *[Loaded](/wiki/Loaded_(British_TV_series) "Loaded (British TV series)")*. In 2019, she played Miss Aitken in [BBC Three](/wiki/BBC_Three "BBC Three")'s comedy series *[Man Like Mobeen](/wiki/Man_Like_Mobeen "Man Like Mobeen")*. In 2022, she joined the cast of *[Slow Horses](/wiki/Slow_Horses "Slow Horses")* during its second season as Shirley Dander.

### Stage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aimee-Ffion_Edwards&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Stage")]

Ffion made her stage debut in 2008 in *SH\*T-M\*X* at the [Trafalgar Theatre](/wiki/Trafalgar_Theatre "Trafalgar Theatre") in London.[[10]](#cite_note-10) In 2009, she performed in *[Jerusalem](/wiki/Jerusalem_(play) "Jerusalem (play)")* at the [Royal Court Theatre](/wiki/Royal_Court_Theatre "Royal Court Theatre").[[6]](#cite_note-WalesOnline-6)[[2]](#cite_note-WalesOnline2-2) In 2011, she made her [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre") debut when she reprised her role in *Jerusalem* at New York's [Music Box Theatre](/wiki/Music_Box_Theatre "Music Box Theatre").[[6]](#cite_note-WalesOnline-6) She appeared in the London revival of *Jerusalem* later that year. In 2012, she appeared in *[The Recruiting Officer](/wiki/The_Recruiting_Officer "The Recruiting Officer")* at the [Donmar Warehouse](/wiki/Donmar_Warehouse "Donmar Warehouse"),[[6]](#cite_note-WalesOnline-6) and in [Marius von Mayenburg](/wiki/Marius_von_Mayenburg "Marius von Mayenburg")'s *Fireface* at the Young Vic.[[11]](#cite_note-11) In 2013, she appeared as Avonia Bunn in *[Trelawny of the Wells](/wiki/Trelawny_of_the_Wells "Trelawny of the Wells")* at the [Donmar Warehouse](/wiki/Donmar_Warehouse "Donmar Warehouse").[[12]](#cite_note-12) In 2017, she played Marcella in *B*, a play by [Guillermo Calderón](/wiki/Guillermo_Calder%C3%B3n "Guillermo Calderón") which had a limited run at the [Royal Court Theatre](/wiki/Royal_Court_Theatre "Royal Court Theatre").

### Video games

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aimee-Ffion_Edwards&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Video games")]

In 2015, Ffion voiced Rachel Baker in the video game *[Everybody's Gone to the Rapture](/wiki/Everybody%27s_Gone_to_the_Rapture "Everybody's Gone to the Rapture")*. In 2022, she voiced [Ranni the Witch](/wiki/Ranni_the_Witch "Ranni the Witch"), a major non-playable character in *[Elden Ring](/wiki/Elden_Ring "Elden Ring")* and Mio in the English dub of *[Xenoblade Chronicles 3](/wiki/Xenoblade_Chronicles_3 "Xenoblade Chronicles 3")*.
